info.name: Zorvyn Finance API
info.description:
# Zorvyn Finance Backend API

A complete Postman collection for all endpoints in the Zorvyn Finance Backend. Built with **FastAPI + MongoDB + JWT**.

## How to Use

1. **Set the `base_url` variable** to `http://localhost:8000/api/v1` (already pre-set).
2. **Run Auth → Register User** to create the first admin account.
3. **Run Auth → Login** — the JWT is automatically saved to the `token` collection variable via a test script.
4. All protected requests use `{{token}}` automatically via Bearer auth.

## Modules
| Module | Base Path | Access |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| Auth | `/auth` | Public |
| Records | `/records` | Viewer+ / Admin |
| Dashboard | `/dashboard` | Viewer+ / Analyst+ |
| Users | `/users` | Viewer (self) / Admin |

## Rate Limits
| Endpoint | Limit |
|----------|-------|
| POST /auth/register | 3/minute |
| POST /auth/login | 5/minute |
| POST /records | 10/minute |
| GET /records | 30/minute |

item.name: 🔐 Auth
item.description: User registration and JWT authentication endpoints. The Login request automatically saves the JWT to the `token` collection variable via a test script.

request.description:
## Register a New User

Creates a new user account. The **first user registered** in the system is automatically promoted to `admin`. All subsequent users default to `viewer`.

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `email` | string | ✅ | Valid email address (must be unique) |
| `password` | string | ✅ | Minimum 8 characters |
| `full_name` | string | ✅ | Display name |

### Response Codes
| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `201` | User created successfully |
| `400` | Email already registered |
| `429` | Rate limit exceeded (3/minute) |

### Notes
- Passwords are bcrypt-hashed before storage — plaintext is never persisted.
- Rate limited to **3 registrations per minute** per IP.

exec: // Automatically extracts and saves the JWT to the collection variable 'token'
// so all subsequent authenticated requests work without manual copy-paste.
console.log("✅ JWT token saved to collection variable 'token'");
console.log("❌ Login failed — token NOT saved. Status:", pm.response.code);

urlencoded.description: Your email address (OAuth2 form uses 'username' field)

request.description:
## Login and Get JWT Token

Authenticates the user and returns a signed JWT access token. This endpoint uses **OAuth2 Password Flow**, so the body must be `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`.

> ✨ **Auto-Token Script**: A test script on this request automatically saves `access_token` to the `token` collection variable. All subsequent requests will use it automatically.

### Request Body (form-urlencoded)
| Field | Value | Description |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| `username` | your email | OAuth2 convention — use your email address here |
| `password` | your password | Plaintext |

### Response Codes
| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `200` | Login successful — JWT returned |
| `401` | Invalid email or password |
| `403` | Account is deactivated |
| `429` | Rate limit exceeded (5/minute) |

### JWT Payload
The token contains:
- `sub`: MongoDB ObjectId of the user
- `role`: One of `admin`, `analyst`, or `viewer`
- `exp`: Expiry timestamp

item.name: 📋 Records
item.description:
Full CRUD for financial records. Supports pagination, filtering by type/category/date, and full-text search on notes/category.

**Role Requirements:**
- `POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`: Admin only
- `GET`: All authenticated roles (viewer, analyst, admin)

request.description:
## Create a Financial Record

Creates a new income or expense record. **Admin only.**

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `amount` | float | ✅ | Monetary value (currency-agnostic) |
| `type` | enum | ✅ | `"income"` or `"expense"` |
| `category` | string | ✅ | E.g. `"salary"`, `"rent"`, `"utilities"` |
| `date` | ISO 8601 datetime | ✅ | Date of the transaction |
| `notes` | string | ❌ | Optional free-text description |

### Response Codes
| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `201` | Record created |
| `401` | Unauthorized — missing/invalid JWT |
| `403` | Forbidden — not an admin |
| `429` | Rate limit exceeded (10/minute) |

### Notes
- Records are **never hard-deleted** (soft-delete pattern). Use the DELETE endpoint to mark as deleted.
- Rate limited to **10 creates per minute**.

query.description: Full-text search on notes and category (regex match)

request.description:
## List Financial Records

Returns a paginated list of financial records. Any authenticated user can call this endpoint.

### Query Parameters
| Param | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `page` | int | `1` | Page number |
| `page_size` | int | `20` | Items per page (max 100) |
| `type` | enum | — | Filter: `income` or `expense` |
| `category` | string | — | Filter by category (exact) |
| `date_from` | datetime | — | Start of date range (ISO 8601) |
| `date_to` | datetime | — | End of date range (ISO 8601) |
| `search` | string | — | Regex search on notes/category |
| `include_deleted` | bool | `false` | Show soft-deleted (Admin only) |

### Disable/Enable Filters
All filters are **disabled by default** in this request. Enable individual params in the **Params** tab as needed.

### Rate Limit
30 requests/minute.

## Get a Single Record

Fetches a specific financial record by its MongoDB ObjectId.

### Path Variables
| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `record_id` | MongoDB ObjectId of the record (e.g. `6613a1b2c3d4e5f678901234`) |

**Tip:** Set `record_id` as a collection variable after creating a record and copy the `id` from the response.

### Response Codes
| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `200` | Record found |
| `404` | Record not found |
| `401` | Unauthorized |

## Update a Financial Record

Partially or fully updates a record. **Admin only.** At least one field must be provided.

### Path Variables
| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `record_id` | MongoDB ObjectId of the record to update |

### Request Body (all fields optional, but at least one required)
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `amount` | float | New monetary value |
| `type` | enum | `"income"` or `"expense"` |
| `category` | string | New category |
| `date` | datetime | New transaction date |
| `notes` | string | New notes text |

### Response Codes
| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `200` | Record updated |
| `400` | No fields provided |
| `404` | Record not found |
| `403` | Not an admin |

## Soft Delete a Record

Marks a record as `is_deleted: true`. **Admin only.**

> ⚠️ **This is NOT a hard delete.** The record remains in MongoDB for audit purposes. Use `include_deleted=true` on the List endpoint (as an admin) to view soft-deleted records.

### Path Variables
| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `record_id` | MongoDB ObjectId of the record to soft-delete |

### Response Codes
| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `204` | Deleted successfully (no body returned) |
| `404` | Record not found |
| `403` | Not an admin |

item.name: 📊 Dashboard
item.description:
Analytics endpoints powered by MongoDB Aggregation Pipelines. All computations happen at the database level for maximum efficiency.

**Role Requirements:**
- `summary`, `category-breakdown`, `recent-activity`: All authenticated roles
- `monthly-trends`: Analyst and Admin only

request.description:
## Dashboard Summary

Returns high-level financial KPIs computed entirely via a MongoDB aggregation pipeline.

### Response Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `total_income` | float | Sum of all non-deleted income records |
| `total_expenses` | float | Sum of all non-deleted expense records |
| `net_balance` | float | `total_income - total_expenses` |
| `total_records` | int | Total count of all non-deleted records |

### Access
All authenticated roles (viewer, analyst, admin).

## Category Breakdown

Groups all records by `category` and `type`, returning the total amount and count per group. Results are sorted by total amount descending.

### Query Parameters
| Param | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `type` | enum | Optional filter: `income` or `expense` |

### Response (array of objects)
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `category` | string | The category name |
| `type` | string | `income` or `expense` |
| `total_amount` | float | Sum of all amounts in this group |
| `count` | int | Number of records in this group |

### Access
All authenticated roles.

query.description: Number of months to look back (default: 12)

request.description:
## Monthly Income & Expense Trends

Returns month-by-month income/expense breakdown for the past N months. Capped at a cutoff date computed server-side. **Analyst and Admin only.**

### Query Parameters
| Param | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `months_back` | int | `12` | How many months of history to return |

### Response (array of monthly objects)
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `year` | int | Calendar year |
| `month` | int | Calendar month (1–12) |
| `income` | float | Total income for the month |
| `expense` | float | Total expenses for the month |
| `net` | float | `income - expense` for the month |

### Access
⚠️ **Analyst and Admin only.** Viewers will receive `403 Forbidden`.

query.description: Max records to return (server caps at 50)

request.description:
## Recent Activity Feed

Returns the most recently created non-deleted records, sorted newest first.

### Query Parameters
| Param | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `limit` | int | `10` | Maximum records to return (server hard-caps at 50) |

### Access
All authenticated roles.

item.name: 👤 Users
item.description:
User management endpoints. Most operations are admin-only. All authenticated users can access their own profile via `/me`.

**Role Requirements:**
- `GET /me`: All authenticated roles
- `GET /`, `PATCH /:id/role`, `PATCH /:id/status`: Admin only

request.description:
## Get Current User Profile

Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user based on the JWT.

### Response Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `id` | string | MongoDB ObjectId |
| `email` | string | User's email |
| `full_name` | string | Display name |
| `role` | enum | `admin`, `analyst`, or `viewer` |
| `is_active` | bool | Whether the account is active |
| `created_at` | datetime | Account creation timestamp |

### Access
All authenticated roles.

## List All Users

Returns all users in the system, sorted by creation date (newest first). **Admin only.**

### Access
⚠️ Admin only.

## Update a User's Role

Promotes or demotes a user's role. **Admin only.**

### Path Variables
| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `user_id` | MongoDB ObjectId of the target user |

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Allowed Values |
|-------|------|----------------|
| `role` | enum | `"admin"`, `"analyst"`, `"viewer"` |

### Business Rules
- An admin **cannot demote themselves** (returns `400`).
- Roles: `viewer < analyst < admin`

### Response Codes
| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `200` | Role updated |
| `400` | Admin self-demotion attempt |
| `404` | User not found |
| `403` | Not an admin |

## Activate or Deactivate a User

Toggles a user's `is_active` status. Deactivated users cannot log in. **Admin only.**

### Path Variables
| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `user_id` | MongoDB ObjectId of the target user |

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `is_active` | bool | `true` to activate, `false` to deactivate |

### Business Rules
- An admin **cannot deactivate themselves** (returns `400`).
- Deactivated users receive `403 Forbidden` on future login attempts.

### Response Codes
| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `200` | Status updated |
| `400` | Admin self-deactivation attempt |
| `404` | User not found |
| `403` | Not an admin |

variable.description: Base URL for the API. Change to your server's address if not running locally.
JWT access token — automatically populated by the Login request's test script.
Set this to the 'id' from any Create Record or List Records response.
Set this to a user's 'id' from the List All Users response.
